incidents.vector: ShinyHunters stole customer records from the company's Salesforce instance in a prior hacking wave and leaked them after an extortion demand was refused.
incidents.impact: Personal details of roughly 13.5 million customers — names, home addresses, emails and phone numbers — were published online.

incidents.victim: P3 Global Intel (US law-enforcement crime-tip platform)
incidents.vector: Hacker known as 'Internet Yiff Machine' exfiltrated the full database from a software vendor used by US police agencies earlier in 2026.
incidents.impact: 8.3 million confidential crime tips containing sensitive informant and investigative data are being offered for sale for $10,000 after initially being shared with journalists; provider had advertised '20 years, 0 breaches'.

incidents.vector: A ransomware operator compromised Autovista's corporate IT estate.
incidents.impact: The attack disrupted Autovista services across Europe and Australia, with the company still working to restore systems.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors, Romanian Air Force, Greek military, Bulgarian and Serbian officials
incidents.vector: Targeted spear-phishing and credential theft against email accounts, with operational data inadvertently exposed on a hacker-run server.
incidents.impact: At least 284 inboxes were compromised between September 2024 and March 2026, including 170+ Ukrainian prosecutors (ARMA, anti-corruption and defense offices), 67 Romanian Air Force accounts, 27 Greek military accounts and officials in Bulgaria and Serbia.

incidents.vector: Intruders breached an internal network segment storing customer data; specific initial-access vector has not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: South Africa's largest bank disclosed customer data was accessed; this is the third reported breach of a South African bank in 2026. Investigation and customer notification ongoing.

incidents.vector: ShinyHunters stole data from Hallmark's Salesforce accounts in a prior campaign and published it after the company refused to pay a ransom.
incidents.impact: Personal information of approximately 1.7 million Hallmark customers was leaked publicly.

incidents.vector: Attackers targeted an operational-technology (OT) control system at the plant; the attempt was blocked by the facility's built-in safeguards.
incidents.impact: The spring-2025 intrusion attempt against a western Sweden heat plant was disclosed publicly this week; no operational outage occurred, but Swedish officials framed it as a shift by pro-Russian groups from DDoS to destructive OT attacks.

incidents.victim: Fake Ledger crypto-wallet app users (Mac App Store)
incidents.vector: A malicious clone of the Ledger wallet app was published to the official Apple Mac App Store and used to steal seed phrases from installed users for about two weeks.
incidents.impact: Roughly $9.5 million was drained from more than 50 victims worldwide, including one musician who lost a $440,000 retirement fund; stolen funds were laundered through KuCoin and a mixer service.

incidents.vector: Highly sophisticated malicious PDF files exploit CVE-2026-34621, a zero-day in Acrobat and Reader, to fingerprint victims and stage follow-on remote code execution and sandbox escape.
incidents.impact: Exploitation has been observed since at least November 2025; Adobe shipped patches on April 11, 2026, but victims during the exposure window had system data silently exfiltrated.

incidents.vector: Breach vector not disclosed; Booking.com detected unauthorized access to customer data and reset PINs for affected reservations.
incidents.impact: Reservation details, names, emails, home addresses and phone numbers of an undisclosed number of customers were exposed; Booking says no financial data was taken.

incidents.vector: Ransomware operators breached county IT networks; this is the second ransomware incident against Winona County in 2026 (separate from the January attack).
incidents.impact: County IT networks and public services were disrupted, prompting Governor Tim Walz to deploy the Minnesota National Guard's cyber units to assist with recovery.

incidents.vector: Attackers gained access to internal systems and published the stolen data online after presumed extortion failed.
incidents.impact: 7.7 TB of highly sensitive material — including LAPD witness names, medical records, internal affairs files and individual officer PII — was leaked publicly.

incidents.vector: Storm-2755 used SEO poisoning and malvertising to drive Canadian users to fake Microsoft 365 sign-in pages, then replayed stolen tokens via an Axios HTTP client to bypass MFA and pivoted to Workday direct-deposit change workflows.
incidents.impact: Microsoft observed compromised Canadian employee accounts used to redirect payroll deposits to attacker-controlled banks, causing direct financial loss for affected individuals and employers.

incidents.vector: Intruders accessed NYK's marine fuel procurement platform; the company took the platform offline.
incidents.impact: Employee data was accessed and likely stolen; the sixth-largest global container shipper (an Ocean Network Express parent) halted use of the bunker fuel system.

incidents.vector: A group calling itself 'FlamingChina' exfiltrated data from NSCC systems earlier in 2026; the volume delayed authentication by weeks.
incidents.impact: Petabytes of NSCC data — including files labeled 'secret' and containing apparent military and government material — were published online.

incidents.victim: 200+ organizations and 5,000+ consumer devices across 120+ countries (foreign ministries, law enforcement, energy, IT/telecom)
incidents.vector: GRU Unit 26165 exploited known vulnerabilities in TP-Link and MikroTik SOHO routers, changed DNS settings to point at attacker-controlled resolvers, then ran adversary-in-the-middle attacks against Outlook Web Access to steal OAuth tokens and session cookies after MFA.
incidents.impact: Over 18,000 routers were hijacked at peak to quietly harvest Microsoft 365 tokens and sensitive traffic. DOJ/FBI 'Operation Masquerade' reset DNS settings on US routers across 23+ states, UK NCSC and German BfV issued parallel advisories, and researchers assess the campaign has ceased.

incidents.victim: US critical infrastructure operators (water, wastewater, energy, local government)
incidents.vector: Iran-affiliated APT targeted internet-exposed Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley programmable logic controllers (including CVE-2021-22681) and Siemens devices, manipulating HMI/SCADA displays and altering project files.
incidents.impact: FBI, NSA, CISA, DOE and Cyber Command jointly warned that intrusions since March 2026 have caused 'operational disruption and financial loss,' including a ransomware-style incident at the Minot, ND water treatment plant and activity against an Indiana county government.

incidents.victim: ChipSoft and Dutch hospitals (HiX electronic health record users)
incidents.vector: Ransomware operators breached ChipSoft's environment; the vendor disabled Zorgportaal, HiX Mobile and the Zorgplatform as a precaution and reissued credentials.
incidents.impact: HiX is used by about 70% of Dutch hospitals; at least 11 hospitals (including Sint Jans Gasthuis, Laurentius, VieCuri and Flevo) temporarily cut ChipSoft connections, LUMC paused an EHR rollout, and the vendor could not rule out patient-data access. No critical care processes stopped.

incidents.vector: Pro-Iranian hacktivist group ran volumetric DDoS attacks against the two consumer platforms.
incidents.impact: Pinterest was offline for a couple of hours and Chime Financial's website was taken down; no data compromise reported. The group framed the attacks around the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

incidents.vector: Silent Ransom Group used a phishing attack to compromise Jones Day personnel, then exfiltrated dated client files and posted them to its leak site on March 30.
incidents.impact: Client materials for 10 Jones Day clients — including files linked to its Federal Circuit appellate practice lead — were posted publicly; all 10 clients have been notified. The firm represented former President Trump in prior campaigns.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, professional services and finance organizations in Australia, UK and US
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware affiliate tracked as Storm-1175 weaponized N-day and zero-day flaws in internet-facing systems — including CVE-2026-23760 in SmarterMail and CVE-2025-10035 in GoAnywhere MFT (exploited a week before public disclosure) — then used RMM tools and Impacket to move to ransomware deployment in as little as 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Recent victims include the University of Mississippi Medical Center and New Jersey's Passaic County government; Microsoft warns the group's tempo and zero-day access make it one of the highest-impact ransomware brands of 2026.

incidents.victim: Users of the Axios NPM package (including OpenAI's Mac apps)
incidents.vector: North Korea-nexus threat actor compromised the widely used Axios NPM HTTP library, injecting malicious code into published package versions that executes on install across downstream projects.
incidents.impact: Widespread downstream impact across enterprise and open-source projects; OpenAI confirmed its Mac apps required emergency updates due to the compromise, and Mandiant/GTIG and Unit 42 both issued threat briefs on the campaign's scope.
